intro: This month focuses on strengthening TLC's liveness checking and XML tooling, consolidating formatter support across the core tools and VS Code, and advancing Apalache's JSON-RPC based exploration workflow. Highlights include integrating the TLA+ formatter into tla2tools, clarifying the deprecation of the Toolbox in favor of VS Code and CLI workflows, and shipping several Apalache releases that add compressed, state focused queries and new exploration primitives.
dev_update_bullets: TLC: Updates the XML exporter schema to enumerate built-in operator names and runs the exporter over all `tlaplus/examples` specs as a regression check, improving documentation and catching schema regressions early ([#1324](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1324)).

TLC: Adds a deprecation notice to the Toolbox welcome page and release notes that directs users to the VS Code and Cursor extensions and CLI usage, clarifying that the Toolbox is no longer actively maintained and pointing to supported workflows ([#1339](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1339)).
TLC: Documents the expected git commit message format in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, making contribution conventions explicit for new contributors ([#1341](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1341)).

TLC: Adds regression tests for an ENABLED soundness bug involving Java-overridden operators and INSTANCE substitution, ensuring TLC no longer silently returns incorrect booleans in this corner case ([#1342](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1342)).

TLC: Extends CI workflows to run on `windows-latest` and adjusts build steps conditionally by OS, improving cross-platform coverage for the toolchain ([#1343](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1343)).

TLC: Refactors architecture detection to use `BIT_32` and `BIT_64` instead of x86-specific labels, fixing incorrect reporting on non-x86 platforms such as ARM and simplifying downstream handling ([#1345](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1345)).

TLC: Expands the README usage section with notes on GUI-based workflows and adds `USE.md` to document using TLC as a Java library or via standard output formats, making integration scenarios more discoverable ([#1340](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1340)).

TLC: Fixes a liveness-checking bug where encountering an accepting tableau node during safety checking could produce a bogus finite-prefix safety counterexample, ensuring PEM conditions are respected before reporting violations ([#1348](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1348)).

TLC: Implements lazy evaluation of operator arguments in `Liveness.java` and adds regression tests for properties defined via parameterized operators in INSTANCEd modules, improving correctness for state-level expressions in liveness checking ([#1350](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1350)).

TLC: Integrates the TLA+ formatter into `tla2tools` so it ships alongside SANY and TLC in `tlatools.jar`, removes web-only dependencies, updates the Ant build, and fixes tests on Windows, making formatting a first-class offline tool ([#1347](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1347)).

TLC: Adds comprehensive documentation comments and TODOs to the XML exporter schema file without changing the schema itself, clarifying the meaning and intended evolution of XML elements ([#1346](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1346)).

TLC: Defers raising `TLC_CONFIG_NO_SPEC_BUT_PROPERTY` and `TLC_CONFIG_NO_FAIRNESS_BUT_LIVE_PROPERTY` until liveness processing can classify violations as safety or liveness, eliminating misleading warnings for pure safety violations ([#1351](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1351)).

TLC: Adds `-suppressMessages` and `-warningsAsErrors` flags to SANY, allowing users to silence specific diagnostic codes or treat selected warnings as errors for stricter CI and editor workflows ([#1344](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1344)).
TLC: Skips example specs that require Apalache in CI, removing an external build-time dependency and stabilizing the test pipeline ([#1354](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1354)).

TLC: Changes SANY to run the linter only after successful semantic analysis and level checking, preventing crashes when semantic analysis is skipped and aligning linting with the most complete analysis phase ([#1356](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1356)).

TLC: Improves the XML exporter so that parse failures only print SANY's user-friendly error messages and use standardized exit codes, avoiding raw Java stack traces and making the tool suitable as TLAPM's main parser ([#1327](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1327)).

TLC: Tightens CI security by removing some third-party GitHub Actions and pinning versions of the remaining ones, reducing supply-chain risk in the build pipeline ([#1364](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1364)).

TLC: Discontinues macOS code signing and notarization due to expired certificates and developer membership, informing users that they must manually whitelist the Toolbox or switch to VS Code or CLI-based workflows ([#1365](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1365)).

TLC: Enhances liveness error reporting to show which temporal property is violated, making counterexamples easier to interpret in models with multiple temporal properties ([#1355](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1355)).

VS Code Extension: Updates the formatter URL to point at its new location, keeping the extension aligned with the formatter now integrated into `tla2tools` ([#506](https://github.com/tlaplus/vscode-tlaplus/pull/506)).

VS Code Extension: Fixes the MCP TLC tools so that an explicit `cfgFile` parameter is honored instead of triggering automatic discovery, enabling workflows with multiple configuration files per module ([#509](https://github.com/tlaplus/vscode-tlaplus/pull/509)).

VS Code Extension: Switches to the formatter bundled in `tlatools`, simplifying installation and ensuring the extension uses the same formatter as the core TLA+ distribution ([#510](https://github.com/tlaplus/vscode-tlaplus/pull/510)).

VS Code Extension: Adjusts tests to expect no linting output after SANY parse failures, matching the new SANY behavior that only lints after successful semantic analysis ([#512](https://github.com/tlaplus/vscode-tlaplus/pull/512)).

VS Code Extension: Updates the CI workflow to mirror the release workflow, so that passing CI is a stronger predictor that releases will succeed ([#511](https://github.com/tlaplus/vscode-tlaplus/pull/511)).

TLAPM: Fixes several example specs that reused identifiers from enclosing scopes, updating them to avoid shadowing so they parse correctly with SANY and match the TLA+ language rules ([#251](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlapm/pull/251)).

Apalache: Releases v0.56.1 with support for translating Quint's `leadsTo` operator to TLA+, fixes LET-IN printing to avoid unintended scope extension, and corrects handling of grouped-variable `UNCHANGED` with nested operator references ([v0.56.1](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/releases/tag/v0.56.1)).

Apalache: Releases v0.56.0 with Jetty 12.1.7 and Jakarta Servlet 6.0, adds gzip and Zstandard compression to the JSON-RPC server, and introduces a `STATE` query kind for retrieving only the last state to reduce response size ([v0.56.0](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/releases/tag/v0.56.0)).

Apalache: Releases v0.55.0 with a new `compact` JSON-RPC method that rewinds to a snapshot and reasserts the last concrete state as a synthetic transition, helping reset solver complexity during long explorations ([v0.55.0](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/releases/tag/v0.55.0)).

Apalache: Releases v0.54.0 as an internal consolidation release, preparing the ground for subsequent JSON-RPC and exploration features ([v0.54.0](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/releases/tag/v0.54.0)).

Apalache: Releases v0.52.3 with an `applyInOrder` JSON-RPC method for batching multiple exploration steps into one request and a lexer fix so tags like `RecvNotification` are tokenized correctly ([v0.52.3](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/releases/tag/v0.52.3)).

Apalache: Simplifies macOS integration-test CI by removing the Nix-based path and installing tools directly, avoiding out-of-memory failures during `nix develop` on GitHub-hosted runners ([#3279](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3279)).

Apalache: Fixes a `Type1Lexer` bug that incorrectly tokenized variant tags like `RecvNotification(...)`, ensuring such identifiers are parsed as single tokens ([#3278](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3278)).

Apalache: Adds the `applyInOrder` JSON-RPC method to group multiple exploration operations into a single server call, reducing client-server round trips and improving interactive performance ([#3280](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3280)).

Apalache: Prepares and merges the 0.52.3 release branch, publishing the new JSON-RPC batching and lexer fixes according to the project's release process ([#3281](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3281)).
Apalache: Prepares and merges the 0.54.0 release branch, bundling recent internal changes into a tagged release ([#3283](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3283)).

Apalache: Implements the `compact` JSON-RPC method that extracts the last concrete state and replays it from a snapshot, with tests and documentation, enabling clients to periodically simplify long-running sessions ([#3285](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3285)).
Apalache: Prepares and merges the 0.55.0 release branch, making the new `compact` exploration capability available to users ([#3286](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3286)).
Apalache: Updates `sbt` and `scripted-plugin` from 1.12.2 to 1.12.6, keeping the build tooling current and benefiting from upstream fixes ([#3277](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3277)).
Apalache: Upgrades `sbt-scalafix` to 0.14.6, aligning the codebase with the latest linting and refactoring tooling ([#3272](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3272)).

Apalache: Upgrades the JSON-RPC server's Jetty dependency to 12.x, modernizing the HTTP stack and enabling newer features such as improved compression handling ([#3289](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3289)).

Apalache: Extends the JSON-RPC `query` method with a `STATE` kind that returns only the last state, reducing payload size when full traces are unnecessary ([#3288](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3288)).

Apalache: Adds gzip and Zstandard compression to the JSON-RPC server using Jetty 12.1.x's `CompressionHandler`, significantly shrinking large JSON responses over the network ([#3290](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3290)).
Apalache: Updates `logback-classic` and `logback-core` to 1.5.32, pulling in logging fixes and improvements from upstream ([#3268](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3268)).
Apalache: Bumps `jackson-module-scala` to 2.20.2, keeping JSON serialization components in sync with the latest patch release ([#3251](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3251)).
Apalache: Updates `scalafmt-core` to 3.10.7, maintaining consistent code formatting with the latest formatter version ([#3265](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3265)).
Apalache: Prepares and merges the 0.56.0 release branch, publishing the Jetty upgrade, compression, and `STATE` query enhancements ([#3291](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3291)).

Apalache: Adds Quint-to-TLA+ conversion for `leadsTo` and fixes a bug in `setBy` transpilation involving LET-IN caching, preventing incorrect evaluation of certain expressions ([#3294](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3294)).

Apalache: Correctly inlines nullary operator definitions in `UNCHANGED` expressions, resolving a bug where grouped-variable `UNCHANGED` could trigger "used before it is assigned" errors ([#3295](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3295)).
Apalache: Updates `sbt` and `scripted-plugin` to 1.12.7, continuing incremental maintenance of the build toolchain ([#3296](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3296)).
Apalache: Prepares and merges the 0.56.1 release branch, delivering `leadsTo` support and recent bug fixes to users ([#3297](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3297)).
Apalache: Updates `sbt` and `scripted-plugin` again to 1.12.8, keeping the build configuration aligned with the latest stable sbt release ([#3298](https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/pull/3298)).
community_bullets: In [“Describing unless condition in lisp”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06687.html), the community discusses how to express TLA+’s `UNLESS` construct in Lisp-like syntax while preserving its temporal meaning.

[“In SANY, what is the APSubstInNode AST node used for?”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06676.html) clarifies the role of `APSubstInNode` in the TLA+ parser’s AST and how it supports substitution in expressions.
The Outreach Committee invites everyone to the next public meeting on January 15 at 11am PT in [“Join us at the next TLA+ Outreach Committee meeting”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06684.html).

[“Leader backs up followers quickly with persistance”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06686.html) explores modeling a Raft-like protocol and how to capture persistence and fast follower catch-up in TLA+.

In [“Model network using set or bag”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06689.html), practitioners compare using sets versus multisets to model message-passing networks and their performance implications in TLC.
[“Request for review?”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06677.html) features a community review of a user’s specification, with feedback on structure, invariants, and proof strategy.
[“Stuttering: abstraction vs. implementation”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06708.html) revisits the role of stuttering steps when relating high-level specifications to more detailed implementations.

[“TLX — TLA+ specifications in Elixir syntax, with TLC integration”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06712.html) announces an experimental project that embeds TLA+ specs in Elixir syntax while remaining compatible with TLC.

The thread [“Using TLA+ to Fix a Very Difficult glibc Bug - Malte Skarupke - C++Now 2025”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06675.html) shares and discusses a talk on applying TLA+ to diagnose and fix a subtle concurrency bug in glibc.
[“What is the plan for Distributed PlusCal?”](https://discuss.tlapl.us/msg06673.html) asks about the roadmap for Distributed PlusCal and receives an update on its current status and future direction.

The TLA+ Foundation announces its [Grant Program Call for Proposals](https://foundation.tlapl.us/grants/2024-grant-program/index.html), offering USD $1,000–$100,000 for projects that advance TLA+ and are typically completed within one year.

Newly funded projects are listed on the [Grant Recipients](https://foundation.tlapl.us/grants/grant-recipients/index.html) page, highlighting work selected for its potential to advance TLA+ technology and benefit the community.
